skill.name: slack-gif-creator
skill.description: Create Slack-ready animated GIFs and emoji animations with reusable Python templates, frame composition tools, and validation helpers for Slack size limits.
skill.summary: Build and validate animated GIFs optimized for Slack messages and custom emoji.
skill.author: ArtemisAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from animation terminology, Markdown code fences, and Apache license text. No evidence found of malware intent, network exfiltration, command execution, prompt injection, or weak cryptographic use. The remaining risk is expected local filesystem access for reading and writing GIF files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Animation and Documentation Keywords
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography, C2, reconnaissance, and blocker matches are natural-language text, animation terms, comments, or validation messages. No evidence found of cryptographic APIs, command-and-control behavior, reconnaissance intent, or malicious logic at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines were reviewed and contain comments, prose, animation labels, or user guidance. They do not contain cryptographic functions, network code, shell execution, or system enumeration logic.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The many Ruby or shell backtick alerts in SKILL.md are Markdown code fences and inline code examples for using the toolkit. The only shell command shown is a normal dependency installation instruction, and no skill file executes it automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed range is documentation with Python examples and one pip install command. There is no subprocess, os.system, eval, exec, or automatic command runner in the cited documentation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: License URLs Are Not Runtime Network Access
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL matches are Apache License reference links. They are not used by runtime code and do not perform network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both cited locations are standard Apache License text. They are static legal references, not executable code paths.

low_findings.title: Expected Local File Read and Write Operations
low_findings.description: The toolkit reads GIF metadata and writes optimized GIF output paths supplied by the user. This filesystem access is central to the skill purpose, but users should avoid untrusted paths or overwriting important files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file access is explicit and easy to trace through Path, imageio.imwrite, Path.stat, and Image.open. It appears legitimate for GIF creation and validation, with no evidence of hidden file collection.

content.value_statement: Slack GIFs often fail because they are too large, too slow, or poorly sized. This skill provides animation templates, image helpers, and validators for Slack message GIFs and custom emoji.
actual_capabilities: Build animated GIFs from generated image frames using Python helpers.
Create Slack emoji animations at 128 by 128 pixels with aggressive size optimization.
Apply reusable animation templates such as bounce, shake, spin, fade, zoom, pulse, and morph.
Compose text, emojis, shapes, gradients, shadows, particles, and visual effects into frames.
Validate GIF file size, dimensions, duration, frame count, and Slack readiness.
Reduce colors, resize frames, remove duplicates, and lower frame counts for smaller files.
limitations: It does not upload GIFs to Slack or manage Slack workspace settings.
Emoji GIFs may still need manual iteration to meet Slack’s 64 KB limit.
It depends on local Python packages such as Pillow, imageio, and numpy.
Complex animations can lose detail when optimized for very small file sizes.

use_cases.title: Team Reaction Emoji
use_cases.description: Create a small animated emoji that matches a team phrase, campaign, or recurring reaction.

use_cases.title: Launch Announcement GIF
use_cases.description: Produce a short celebratory message GIF for Slack announcements without exceeding common file limits.

use_cases.title: Status and Alert Animation
use_cases.description: Design attention-grabbing Slack visuals for incidents, reminders, and workflow updates.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Emoji GIF
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a custom Slack emoji.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 128 by 128 Slack emoji GIF of a smiling face pulsing twice. Keep it under 64 KB and validate the result.

prompt_templates.title: Make a Message Reaction GIF
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a message-sized GIF.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Slack message GIF that says “Ship it” with a bounce effect, confetti, and a clean background. Optimize it for Slack messages.

prompt_templates.title: Combine Multiple Effects
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Slack GIF where a warning emoji slides in, shakes, then fades out. Use reusable templates and report size, frame count, and duration.

prompt_templates.title: Iterate for Strict Size Limits
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a custom emoji GIF for Slack, then iteratively reduce frames, colors, and visual complexity until it passes the 64 KB limit.

output_examples.input: Create a pulsing heart emoji for Slack.
output_examples.output: A compact 128 by 128 animated heart GIF with a heartbeat pulse, reduced colors, and Slack emoji size validation results.

output_examples.input: Make a celebration GIF for a product launch.
output_examples.output: A short Slack message GIF with animated text, confetti particles, optimized color palette, and a size summary for upload readiness.

output_examples.input: Create an alert animation for an incident channel.
output_examples.output: A clear warning emoji animation with shake and fade effects, designed for fast recognition in Slack.

best_practices: Start with fewer frames and colors when targeting Slack custom emoji.
Validate size and dimensions after every major animation change.
Use simple shapes, solid colors, and clear motion for small emoji GIFs.
anti_patterns: Do not use complex gradients or photo-like detail for 64 KB emoji GIFs.
Do not assume a message GIF will also work as a custom emoji.
Do not save output over important files without checking the target path.

faq.question: Does this skill upload GIFs to Slack?
faq.answer: No. It creates and validates GIF files. You upload the finished file through Slack.

faq.question: Can it create Slack custom emoji GIFs?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes 128 by 128 emoji workflows and optimization helpers for Slack’s strict size limit.

faq.question: What dependencies are required?
faq.answer: The toolkit expects Python with Pillow, imageio, and numpy available in the local environment.

faq.question: Why is the 64 KB emoji limit difficult?
faq.answer: Animated GIFs grow quickly with frame count, colors, gradients, and visual complexity.

faq.question: Can I combine animation effects?
faq.answer: Yes. The templates are designed as composable primitives for motion, timing, text, shapes, and effects.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit found no malicious behavior. It uses expected local file access to read and write GIF files.
